payload.answer: Treat this as a temporal status transition on a narrower scope: the MVP is now implemented, while the broader Brain Slack agent project was previously planned/open. Do not present the old planned/open state as current for the MVP, and do not fully overwrite the broader project status unless the system models MVP completion as project completion.
citations: Existing: "The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open."
New: "The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented."

entity_resolution.reason: The new statement refers to the MVP of the Brain Slack agent, which is related to but not strictly identical with the broader project. This supports a milestone-level implementation update without fully replacing the broader project's prior open/planned status.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent MVP is implemented.
The Brain Slack agent was previously planned with status open.

details: Detected a status change over time.
Detected scope difference between parent project and MVP milestone.
Preserved historical status as prior rather than current.
Avoided overstating that the entire project is implemented.

repair_options: Record the MVP as implemented and keep the parent project as a separate entity with its last known broader status.
If project and MVP are modeled as the same entity in this system, update status from open/planned to implemented and archive the old status as historical.
Request clarification on whether MVP implementation means the entire Brain Slack agent project is implemented.
